From Marlon: 

Hi Sam,

As discussed earlier this week, please see attached Rio Tinto quote template.

I’m happy to catch up with you or your team to run through the key line items that would ideally need to pull through.

One thing that I think we need to consider for the Rio Tinto works and how Humanz manages these works is Rio’s track system.

We spent 3 hours with RTIO yesterday running through Track and Kayla mentioned that Humanz can do a Track upload.

I’m assuming that for this to work in Rio’s system the line items/naming conventions would need to be the same?

If so, then the attached quote template will likely need to be as well.

How it Works for Leave: When you configure multiple approvers, the request follows a specific sequence. For example, a request can be routed from a direct manager to a department head and then to an HR administrator.
* Sequential Order: Each approver must sign off before the request moves to the next person in the chain.
* Progress Visibility: Both the employee and administrators can view a progress bar on the request to see who has already approved it and who it is currently waiting on.
* Mobile Support: Approvers can review, comment on, and approve or deny leave directly through the BambooHR mobile app.
Steps to Set it Up* Open Settings: Click the gear icon in the top right corner.
* Select Time Off: Go to the Time Off section and choose Policies.
* Edit the Policy: Select the specific leave policy (e.g., Vacation or Sick Leave) you want to modify.
* Assign Approvers: In the Approvers section, you can add multiple specific individuals or roles (like "Manager" or "Manager's Manager").
* Save: Ensure you save the policy to apply the multi-step workflow.

At this stage, progress is on hold mainly due to two key issues that directly impact the maintenance team:

comments.taskTitleFromUpdateSheet: Look into the functionality of Projects in MEx
comments.body:
* Question - What is the use of Projects in MEX?* A Standard Job is a template for work that is repetitive but unpredictable. When you select a work order and "Raise Std Job," you're creating a new work order from a pre-defined template that contains all standard information for that type of job.How It Works:A Standard Job template includes:* Job instructionsJob instructions
* Safety notes
* Required spare parts
* Task lists
* Estimated hours
* All other job details

When work needs to be done (triggered manually or by inspection answer), you:

* Look up the Standard Job
* Click "Raise Std Job"
* A new Work Order is created with all the template details
* Every work order created from that Standard Job is uniform and consistent

* Real-World Example:Changing tyres on a crane:
* It's repetitive (happens multiple times)
* But unpredictable (not scheduled, happens as needed)
* A Standard Job template ensures every time you do it, you get the same parts, steps, and safety notes

comments.taskTitleFromUpdateSheet: Can we link Multiple WO's to 1 PO?
comments.body:
* Question - Can we assign multiple WO's to 1 PO?* Not out of the box no. We can add multiple PO's in 1 WO but it's not designed to do the other way around.
* A work around would be that we create a Parent WO that is linked to 1 PO and then you have all the Child WO's linked to the parent. This would let you see all the WO's consolidated but only have the PO in the Master.
* Would have to trial this one to see any limitations, not a lot of info.
